title: Add a new example that shows how to chain modules together
body:
How may we help - what is your question?

I have written an example that shows how to use multiple modules to iteratively calculate a value (pi). Unsure of the usefulness on the HW side so thought I would ask first before making a pull request. The example uses "real" types and I believe this is only for simulation. The sim_main.cpp code is below and no worries if this example is not needed. (The .v files do not attach here so pasting below.) 

Let me  know if you want me to make a pull request for review? If not, I can close this issue off. Thanks.

[sim_main.cpp](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26441533/sim_main.cpp)

Makefile line: $(VERILATOR) -cc --exe --build -f input.vc top.v sim_main.cpp
input.vc: same as in other example directories

top.v
```
module top (
    real divisor,
    real sum_in,
    output real sum_out
);

    real intermediate_result;
    
    div div1(/*AUTOINST*/
        .a(4.0),
        .b(divisor),
        .result(intermediate_result)
    );
    
    add add1(/*AUTOINST*/
        .a(sum_in),
        .b(intermediate_result),
        .sum(sum_out)
    );

endmodule
```

add.v
```
module add (
    real a,
    real b,
    output real sum
);

  always @(*) begin
    sum = a + b;
  end
  
endmodule
```

div.v
```
module div (
    real a,
    real b,
    output real result
);

  always @(*) begin
    result = 0;
    if (b) begin
        result = a / b;
    end
  end

endmodule
```
